title: A targeted radiation-hardening update for one CubeSat power-control board
abstract: We present a shielding update for one CubeSat power-control board and show a 12% reduction in latch-up events relative to the current baseline under proton irradiation. Figure 1 shows the reduction, Table 1 reports uncertainty intervals, and the discussion identifies the failure regime above 42 krad. The contribution is narrow but technically sound for specialist spacecraft electronics work.
manuscript_text: We irradiated one CubeSat power-control board configuration in a controlled proton beam campaign and compared the modified shield stack against the current baseline design. Figure 1 reports latch-up reduction across three runs, and Table 1 gives uncertainty intervals for each estimate. The method is limited above 42 krad, where the board still fails. We position the result against prior board-level radiation-hardening references and describe the exact scope boundary for the updated design.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Latch-up reduction for the updated shield stack relative to the baseline design across three proton-beam runs.
tables: Table 1. Latch-up counts and uncertainty intervals for the baseline and updated board configurations.
references: Ref3 uncertainty handling for latch-up testing.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A details beam exposure duration, board revision, and shield dimensions.
data_availability: Beam-test logs are archived in the supplementary materials.
code_availability: Board-analysis scripts are archived with the irradiation logs.
materials_availability: Shield dimensions and board revision notes are listed in Appendix A.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
